sense.definition: a metal framework with bars or slats that covers an opening on a vehicle's exterior, usually for protection or looks

examples.x: The concept car boasts a futuristic, aerodynamically efficient grille.
The luxury SUV comes with an illuminated grille that lights up at night.
The grille of the truck is rugged and durable, suitable for off-road adventures.
A custom-made mesh grille was installed on the sports car to improve airflow to the engine.

sense.definition: a metal part that covers the engine of a vehicle

examples.x: He popped the hood of the car to check the engine after it started making strange noises.
The mechanic lifted the hood to inspect the damage from the minor accident.
She slammed the hood shut after filling up the windshield washer fluid.
A layer of dust had accumulated on the hood, indicating the car hadn't been driven in months.
The hood of the sports car gleamed under the showroom lights, showcasing its pristine condition.

sense.definition: a type of light on a vehicle designed to improve visibility in foggy conditions

examples.x: Fog lamps are typically mounted low on the front bumper of vehicles.
Using fog lamps in foggy weather helps drivers see the road markings and other vehicles better.
Some drivers mistakenly use fog lamps in clear weather, which can be distracting to others on the road.
It's important to switch off fog lamps when visibility improves to avoid dazzling drivers coming from the opposite direction.
My car's fog lamp helps me see better when it's foggy outside.

sense.definition: one of the two powerful, large, and bright lights that are placed at the front of vehicles

examples.x: The car’s headlights were shining brightly in the dark.
I need to replace a headlight on my car.
She turned on her headlights when the sun started setting.
The mechanic checked the headlights to ensure they were working properly.
The left headlight isn't working, so I’ll fix it tomorrow.

sense.definition: the bright headlights on a vehicle used for maximum illumination of the road ahead, especially in dark or low-visibility conditions

examples.x: He switched on the high beams to navigate through the dense fog.
High beams are essential for driving safely on unlit country roads at night.
The driver was reminded to dim his high beams when approaching oncoming traffic.
The car's high beams automatically adjust based on the surrounding light conditions.
She adjusted the angle of the high beams to improve visibility around sharp turns.

sense.definition: the headlights on a vehicle that provide a standard level of illumination for driving under normal conditions, designed to prevent blinding other drivers

examples.x: The driver switched from high beam to low beam when entering the city limits.
Low beams are typically used in urban areas with street lighting.
He adjusted the headlights to ensure the low beams were correctly aligned.
The vehicle's low beams provide adequate visibility without causing glare to oncoming traffic.
The low beams on his motorcycle were bright enough for night riding in suburban areas.

sense.definition: the large front window of a vehicle

examples.x: The windshield was cracked after the stone hit it during the drive.
I had to clean the windshield because it was covered in bugs.
A bird flew into the windshield, causing a loud noise.
He wiped the snow off the windshield before starting the car.
The mechanic replaced the windshield after the accident.

sense.definition: a long and thin device with rubber on its edge, designed to move across the glass at the front of vehicles to clear it of rain, snow, etc. so that the driver can see the road properly

examples.x: She turned on the windshield wiper to clear the heavy rain from the windshield during the storm.
He adjusted the speed of the windshield wiper to match the intensity of the falling snow.
She used the windshield wiper to remove the morning frost from the windshield before starting her commute.
The windshield wiper squeaked as it moved across the glass, indicating it needed some maintenance.

sense.definition: a bar that is attached to the back and front of a vehicle to reduce damage in time of an accident

examples.x: She noticed a small dent on the rear bumper from a previous accident.
The bumper was equipped with a protective cover to prevent scratches and dents.
They added a custom bumper to the truck for a more rugged appearance.
The mechanic inspected the bumper for any hidden damage after the crash.
The collision caused the bumper to pop off, requiring immediate repair.

sense.definition: a light at the rear of a vehicle that is usually red in color

examples.x: He adjusted the tail light to make it brighter for visibility.
The tail light of the car was broken after the accident.
The mechanic checked the tail light along with the other lights.
The tail light flashed when the driver applied the brakes.
She accidentally broke the tail light when loading groceries into the trunk.

sense.definition: a rear-facing light on a vehicle that illuminates when the driver applies the brakes, signaling to other drivers that the vehicle is slowing down or stopping

examples.x: The brake light on his car was not working, so he had it replaced immediately.
She noticed the brake lights of the car ahead come on suddenly, indicating a quick stop.
The truck's brake lights were bright and easily visible even in daylight.
The mechanic inspected the wiring to diagnose the intermittent brake light issue.
The brake lights flashed repeatedly as the driver navigated a steep downhill slope.

sense.definition: the blinking lights on a vehicle used to indicate that the vehicle is stopped or moving slowly due to an emergency or dangerous situation

examples.x: When there is heavy fog, it's important to turn on your hazard lights so other drivers can see your car clearly.
If your car breaks down on the highway, it's safer to turn on the hazard lights until help arrives.
Drivers should use hazard lights cautiously, as they are meant for emergencies like sudden stops or accidents.
During heavy rainstorms, using hazard lights can help prevent accidents by alerting other drivers to slow down.
It's illegal in many places to drive with hazard lights on during normal conditions because it can confuse other drivers.

sense.definition: a streamlined projection, usually found at the rear of a car

examples.x: The classic sports car featured a prominent fin that enhanced its retro design.
The concept car's fin was designed to reduce drag and improve aerodynamics.
The tuner modified the hatchback with a custom carbon fiber fin for racing.
In the 1950s, fins on American cars became a symbol of automotive design innovation.
Many luxury sedans incorporate a subtle fin into their rear spoiler for added style.

sense.definition: an aerodynamic device installed on the rear of a vehicle, designed to reduce lift and increase stability at higher speeds

examples.x: The sports car's rear spoiler improved its handling by providing additional downforce.
She added a carbon fiber spoiler to her sedan to enhance its appearance and performance.
A spoiler is often used in conjunction with other aerodynamic enhancements to optimize airflow.
The aftermarket spoiler on his coupe matched the vehicle's color scheme perfectly.
Drivers appreciate spoilers for their practical benefits as well as their sporty aesthetics.

sense.definition: a type of camera installed on the rear of a vehicle to assist the driver in backing up or reversing by providing a view of the area behind the vehicle
a piece of rubber or plastic that hangs behind the wheels of a vehicle to prevent mud and water from splashing onto other vehicles or pedestrians

examples.x: The truck had big mudflaps that kept the road clean by stopping mud from flying up.
She noticed her bicycle didn't have mudflaps, so her back got dirty after riding through a puddle.
When it rained heavily, the cars without mudflaps made a mess on the road, causing slippery conditions.
The new law requires all trucks to have mudflaps installed to reduce the amount of mud and debris on highways.
After installing mudflaps on his SUV, John found that his car stayed cleaner and required less frequent washing.

sense.definition: the rear door of a car, truck, or van that can be opened downwards when loading or unloading goods

examples.x: He opened the tailgate of his pickup truck to unload bags of soil for the garden.
The delivery driver lowered the tailgate of the van to unload packages for the office.
They sat on the tailgate of the SUV, enjoying a picnic lunch at the scenic overlook.
With the tailgate down, they loaded the camping gear into the back of the Jeep.
She closed the tailgate of her hatchback after loading groceries into the trunk.

sense.definition: a device on a vehicle that flashes to signal the intention of the driver to change lanes or make a turn

examples.x: He activated the blinker to signal his intention to merge into the right lane.
The driver forgot to turn off the blinker after making a left turn.
The motorcycle's blinker was flashing rapidly, indicating a malfunction.
In heavy traffic, drivers rely on blinkers to anticipate lane changes and turns.
He adjusted the sensitivity of the blinker control for smoother signaling.

sense.definition: an electrical part in a vehicle that manages the blinking of turn signals and emergency lights

examples.x: A clicking sound from the flasher indicated that the hazard lights were operational.
The driver activated the hazard flashers to alert other motorists of a sudden stop on the highway.
The technician inspected the wiring connected to the flasher to troubleshoot the intermittent issue.

sense.definition: a light on a vehicle that blinks to indicate a change in lane

examples.x: Before merging into the next lane, she activated her turn signal to alert other drivers.
The driver forgot to use the turn signal, causing a nearby car to slow down abruptly.
The turn signal light on the dashboard flashed, indicating the left turn was being made.
It’s important to use your turn signal well in advance to give other drivers enough notice.
The mechanic checked the turn signal wiring to fix the issue of the lights not blinking.

sense.definition: a metal or plastic plate on a vehicle, displaying a unique combination of letters and numbers assigned by the government for identification

examples.x: The police officer ran the license plate number through the database to check for any outstanding warrants.
He ordered personalized license plates for his car with his initials and favorite number.
The vintage car enthusiast restored the original license plates from the vehicle's production year.
The stolen car was identified by its license plate number captured on a surveillance camera.
A custom license plate frame with the university logo was a gift from her alma mater.

sense.definition: a small light mounted on a vehicle's rear to illuminate its license plate

examples.x: My car's license plate lamp stopped working, so I had to replace it with a new bulb.
The police officer noticed that the license plate lamp was broken and advised me to fix it promptly.
In some countries, having a functioning license plate lamp is a legal requirement for driving at night.
During the vehicle inspection, they checked the license plate lamp to ensure it was in good working condition.
When parking in dark areas, the license plate lamp helps others see the vehicle's registration number clearly.

sense.definition: a reflective device attached to the side of a vehicle, allowing the driver to see what is behind and beside them

examples.x: Before changing lanes, it's important to check your wing mirrors for any approaching vehicles.
The driver adjusted the wing mirror to get a better view of the parking space behind the car.
She noticed a scratch on the edge of her wing mirror after parking too close to the curb.
It's helpful to keep your wing mirrors clean to ensure clear visibility while driving.
When parallel parking, use your wing mirrors to gauge the distance from the curb.

sense.definition: a part of a car's bodywork that covers the area between the rear door and the trunk

examples.x: The mechanic repaired the dent on the quarter panel of my car after the accident.
She noticed a scratch on the quarter panel while washing her car.
The cost to replace a damaged quarter panel can be quite expensive.
He painted the new quarter panel to match the original color of the vehicle.
The collision caused damage to both the bumper and the quarter panel of the car.

sense.definition: a part that helps balance and stabilize the car while driving

examples.x: The rocker on my uncle's classic car was painted in shiny silver to match its vintage style.
When the car hit a bump, I noticed the rocker moved slightly, absorbing some of the impact.
Mechanics often check the condition of the rocker during routine maintenance to ensure it's secure and functioning properly.
The repair shop replaced the damaged rocker with a new one after the accident.
A strong rocker is important for maintaining stability, especially during sharp turns or sudden stops.

sense.definition: a type of car roof that has removable panels over the driver's and passenger's seats

examples.x: The classic sports car had a T-top roof, allowing for an open-air driving experience on sunny days.
He loved the T-top design of his car because he could enjoy the breeze without fully removing the roof panels.
When it rained suddenly, they hurried to put the T-top panels back on to keep the interior dry.
The T-top gave the car a distinctive look, making it stand out among other vehicles on the road.

sense.definition: a part of a vehicle's roof that can slide open to have more light or ventilation

examples.x: She opened the sunroof to enjoy the fresh air during the drive.
The car has a sunroof that lets in natural light.
They drove with the sunroof open to watch the stars.
The sunroof leaked during the heavy rain.
He closed the sunroof after realizing it was too windy.

sense.definition: a type of sunroof that is specifically made of glass, allowing natural light to enter the vehicle even when closed

examples.x: My new car has a moonroof, so I can enjoy the fresh air while driving.
During our road trip, we loved looking at the stars through the moonroof at night.
She always keeps the moonroof closed when it's raining to avoid getting wet inside the car.
The moonroof in his vehicle makes it feel more spacious and airy.
They installed a new moonroof in the car to make summer drives more enjoyable.

sense.definition: a loud device typically used on emergency vehicles to alert others of their approach and to clear the way in traffic

examples.x: The ambulance's siren wailed as it sped through the busy intersection.
Fire trucks use a powerful siren to warn pedestrians and vehicles to make way.
The police car's siren was activated during the high-speed chase.
The rescue team's boat was equipped with a siren for use during water emergencies.

sense.definition: a set of bars or a frame mounted on the roof of a vehicle to carry additional cargo, such as luggage, bicycles, or sports equipment

examples.x: The roof rack allowed them to carry extra luggage for their road trip.
They used the roof rack to carry the Christmas tree home from the farm.
He installed a roof rack on his SUV to transport his mountain bikes.
She secured the kayak to the roof rack before heading to the lake.
The roof rack made it easy to transport the surfboards to the beach.

sense.definition: a device mounted on vehicles to receive radio signals for entertainment and communication purposes

examples.x: The car antenna can be vulnerable to damage in car washes or when passing through low-clearance areas.
It's important to extend the car antenna fully to optimize radio reception while driving in remote areas.
Mechanics often replace car antennas if they become bent or fail to pick up radio signals effectively.
In some regions, people customize their car antennas with decorative covers or flags for personal style.
The car antenna is typically located at the front or rear of the vehicle to ensure optimal signal reception regardless of its orientation on the road.

sense.definition: a narrow step fitted under the side doors of a vehicle, typically a truck or SUV, to assist passengers in entering and exiting the vehicle

examples.x: He installed a running board on his truck to make it easier for his kids to climb in.
She appreciated the running board on her new vehicle, especially when wearing heels.
The running boards on the vintage car were polished to a shine for the car show.
The running boards matched the truck's chrome trim, adding a stylish touch.
The running board helped elderly passengers get in and out of the high vehicle more comfortably.

sense.definition: an exterior mirror mounted on the side of a vehicle, typically near the driver and passenger doors, providing visibility to the sides and rear of the vehicle

examples.x: He adjusted the side-view mirrors to eliminate blind spots before starting the car.
The side-view mirror on the passenger side was cracked and needed replacement.
The car's side-view mirrors automatically folded when parked to prevent damage.
She glanced at the side-view mirror to check for approaching traffic before changing lanes.

sense.definition: the lid on a vehicle's fuel tank used to seal the opening

examples.x: I need to open the filler cap on my car to refuel it.
Make sure to tighten the filler cap securely after filling up the tank.
The mechanic checked the filler cap to see if it was causing the fuel leak.
You can find the location of the filler cap in the owner's manual of your car.
If the filler cap is damaged, it might lead to problems with the fuel system.

sense.definition: the curved panel located above each wheel of a vehicle, designed to cover and protect the wheel well from road debris and provide aerodynamic flow

examples.x: The classic car's fenders were made of steel and meticulously restored to their original condition.
He accidentally scratched the fender of his truck while maneuvering into a tight parking spot.
The manufacturer redesigned the car's front fender to enhance its sporty appearance.
The vintage motorcycle's fender was chrome-plated for a shiny finish.
A dent in the rear fender was caused by a minor collision in a parking lot.

sense.definition: the central part of a wheel where the axle is housed, allowing the wheel to rotate freely

examples.x: The mechanic inspected the hub bearings to ensure smooth wheel rotation.
The racing team upgraded the car's hubs to improve handling and performance.
The hub of the rear wheels showed signs of wear and needed replacement.
The manufacturer recommends greasing the hub bearings during routine maintenance.
The auto shop specializes in refurbishing vintage car hubs for restoration projects.

sense.definition: a decorative cover placed over the central portion of a vehicle's wheel

examples.x: He accidentally scratched the hubcap while parallel parking in a tight space.
The hubcaps on the fleet of delivery vans were replaced with durable plastic covers.
The vintage car's hubcaps were polished to a mirror-like shine for the car show.
The mechanic recommended replacing the rusted hubcaps to enhance the car's appearance.

sense.definition: a tool used to measure the amount of air in a tire

examples.x: Before going on a long trip, I always use a tire pressure gauge to check my car's tires.
The mechanic showed me how to use the tire pressure gauge to make sure my tires are safe.
You can buy a tire pressure gauge at most auto parts stores for a low price.
I forgot to bring my tire pressure gauge, so I had to stop at a gas station to check the tires.
It's important to use a tire pressure gauge regularly to avoid any problems with your car's tires.

sense.definition: a thick wire with metal clips used to connect a dead car battery to a charged one to help start the engine

examples.x: He kept a set of jumper cables in his trunk in case his battery ever died.
The car would not start, so she asked a neighbor for help with jumper cables.
Before using the jumper cables, he made sure to connect them in the right order.
Her battery was completely dead, so they had to use jumper cables to start the car.
A passerby offered to help by connecting jumper cables to his working battery.

sense.definition: an extra tire kept in a vehicle for use in case one of the main tires becomes flat or damaged

examples.x: He pulled over to change the flat tire with the spare tire from the trunk.
The rental car came equipped with a compact spare tire for emergencies.
It's important to check the air pressure of the spare tire regularly to ensure it's ready for use.
She used the jack and lug wrench to lower the spare tire from underneath the truck.
He stored an emergency kit with tools and a flashlight near the spare tire in the trunk.

sense.definition: a part made for a machine or vehicle that can be used to replace an old or broken part

examples.x: He always keeps a spare part for his car in case something needs to be replaced.
The mechanic ordered a spare part to fix the broken engine.
The factory has a storage room filled with spare parts for quick repairs.
She could not fix the washing machine because the spare part was out of stock.
He bought a spare part online after noticing wear on the old one.

sense.definition: a small metal nut that secures a wheel to a vehicle
a pipe through which harmful gasses exit from a car

examples.x: The mechanic examined the tailpipe for signs of exhaust leaks or corrosion.
He noticed black smoke coming from the tailpipe, indicating a potential engine problem.
The car emitted a loud noise from the tailpipe, suggesting an issue with the muffler.
The tailpipe emissions are regulated to reduce air pollution and improve air quality.
She installed a new chrome-plated tailpipe to enhance the appearance of her vehicle.

sense.definition: the space at the back of an automobile in which different things can be put

examples.x: They packed their suitcases in the trunk of the car before heading to the airport.
She stored her sports equipment in the trunk of her SUV for easy access.
The grocery bags filled the trunk after a trip to the supermarket.
He opened the trunk to retrieve the spare tire for a roadside tire change.
The trunk space in the sedan was spacious enough for all their camping gear.

sense.definition: the curved structure around a wheel on a vehicle

examples.x: The car's wheel arches were designed to accommodate large tires for off-road driving.
When I hit a pothole, I heard a loud noise coming from the wheel arch.
The mechanic found rust forming inside the wheel arches, which needed immediate repair.
She admired the sleek design of the sports car, especially its pronounced wheel arches.
The mud on the wheel arches showed evidence of the car's recent adventure through the countryside.

sense.definition: a one-wheeled or two-wheeled attachment to a motorcycle or bicycle, positioned to the side and designed to carry passengers or cargo

examples.x: The sidecar was equipped with a windshield and comfortable seating for the passenger.
They toured the countryside on a vintage motorcycle with a classic sidecar attached.
He installed a custom sidecar on his motorcycle to transport his dog on rides.
The sidecar's design complemented the retro styling of the motorcycle.

sense.definition: a part at the front or back of a car designed to absorb the energy of a crash and protect the people inside

examples.x: The crumple zone in a car helps to keep passengers safe during an accident.
Engineers design the crumple zone to collapse easily when the car hits something.
During a crash test, the crumple zone of the vehicle was carefully examined.
Modern cars have a crumple zone that can reduce injuries in a collision.
The crumple zone is an important feature in making cars safer on the road.
